title: Fix mobile layout alignment on Android devices

requirements.text: Fix the mobile (Android) layout alignment issue where content is shifted to the right. Ensure all page content is properly centered on Android devices in normal browsing mode while preserving the existing desktop layout.

quotes: in the Android normal browsing mode, the website is slightly aligned to the right side
align the website to center in normal Android mode

acceptanceCriteria: All content is centered on Android mobile devices
No horizontal overflow or right-side shift occurs on mobile viewports
Desktop layout remains unchanged and properly centered
All interactive elements (buttons, forms, cards) are accessible without horizontal scrolling

requirements.text: Review and fix responsive layout styles in index.css and component-level styles to eliminate any CSS rules causing right-alignment or horizontal overflow on mobile devices. Ensure proper viewport meta tag configuration and mobile-first responsive breakpoints.

quotes: makes some things uncomfortable and some things terrible

acceptanceCriteria: Mobile viewport is properly configured with correct meta tags
All containers use proper responsive width constraints (max-w-full, container classes)
No fixed-width elements cause horizontal overflow on narrow screens
Touch targets meet minimum size requirements for mobile usability

constraints: Do not modify the desktop layout or styling
Preserve all existing component functionality and interactivity
Maintain the dark theme and glassmorphism design aesthetic
nonGoals: Redesigning the mobile UI beyond fixing alignment
Changing the color scheme or visual design
